story.iWant: all functions to return pipeline-compatible objects
story.soThat: users can chain functions in custom workflows
story.tasks: - Define return object models (AC: #1, #2, #3) - Create DateRangeResult record (DONE in Story 1.1) - Create LogFileInfo record - Create LogFolderInfo record - Create ArchiveInfo record - Ensure all records are PowerShell-compatible - Implement ValueFromPipeline parameters (AC: #4) - Add ValueFromPipeline to Find-LogFiles DateRange parameter - Add ValueFromPipeline to Find-LogFolders DateRange parameter - Add ValueFromPipeline to Test-ArchiveExists DateRange parameter - Add ValueFromPipeline to Compress-Logs SourcePath parameter - Create pipeline integration tests (AC: #4, #5) - Test Get-DateRange | Find-LogFolders pipeline - Test Get-DateRange | Test-ArchiveExists | Find-LogFolders pipeline - Test Find-LogFolders | Compress-Logs pipeline - Create documentation examples

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. Define return object schemas for each function type 2. All functions output PSCustomObject or equivalent 3. Objects include essential properties for next pipeline stage 4. Pipeline chaining validated with example workflows 5. Documentation shows pipeline composition examples

docs.doc: - Get-DateRange output piped to Find-LogFolders → Find-LogFolders accepts DateRangeResult[] from pipeline - Get-DateRange → Test-ArchiveExists → Find-LogFolders → filters dates through entire chain - Find-LogFolders output piped to Compress-Logs → Compress-Logs accepts LogFolderInfo[] from pipeline
All return types must serialize properly for PowerShell. PowerShell pipeline compatibility requires [Parameter(ValueFromPipeline = true)] attribute.
Foundation layer framework. Cross-cutting concern for all functions. All return types must be C# record types (modern .NET pattern).

artifact.reason: ALREADY IMPLEMENTED - Example pattern for other record types (LogFileInfo, LogFolderInfo, ArchiveInfo)
Example cmdlet structure showing OutputType attribute - other cmdlets should follow

constraints.constraint: Foundation layer framework - affects all cmdlets
All return types MUST be C# record types with init-only properties (modern .NET pattern per ADR-001)
Records must be immutable (init-only setters, no mutable state)
PowerShell compatibility: [Parameter(ValueFromPipeline = true)] for pipeline inputs
Properties must serialize correctly for PowerShell (avoid complex nested objects)
DateRangeResult already exists (Story 1.1) - reuse, don't recreate
Use .NET FileInfo and DirectoryInfo types where appropriate
Provide complete XML documentation comments for all public models

tests.standards: Unit tests: xUnit for model validation logic (if any). Integration tests: Pester (PowerShell) for pipeline chaining validation. Test naming: MethodName_Scenario_ExpectedResult (xUnit), Describe/Context/It (Pester)
tests.locations: src/logManager/Models/LogFileInfo.cs src/logManager/Models/LogFolderInfo.cs src/logManager/Models/ArchiveInfo.cs tests/logManager.Tests/Integration/Pipeline-Composition.Tests.ps1 docs/USAGE.md (pipeline examples section)

ideas.test: Create LogFileInfo record with FileInfo, LogDate, DateSource properties
Create LogFolderInfo record with DirectoryInfo, ParsedDate, FolderNamePattern properties
Create ArchiveInfo record with ArchiveFile, CompressionEngine, sizes, duration properties
Verify all records use init-only properties (immutable)
Pester test: Get-DateRange | Find-LogFolders pipeline works
Pester test: Get-DateRange | Test-ArchiveExists | Find-LogFolders multi-stage pipeline
Test DateRangeResult[] flows correctly through pipeline to Find-LogFiles
Test LogFolderInfo[] flows correctly to Compress-Logs
Create USAGE.md documentation with 3+ pipeline composition examples
Verify models serialize/deserialize correctly in PowerShell (Export-Clixml, Import-Clixml)
